message:
feat(core): intelligent snapshot scheduling with trigger-value accumulation (wa-3kxe)

Extends SnapshotEngine.run_periodic() to support two scheduling modes:

**Periodic mode** (existing behavior, refactored):
  Fixed-interval captures with startup snapshot on first tick.

**Intelligent mode** (new):
  Event-driven scheduling that accumulates trigger values from
  SnapshotTrigger variants (WorkCompleted=2.0, StateTransition=1.0,
  IdleWindow=3.0, MemoryPressure=4.0, HazardThreshold=10.0) and
  fires a capture when the accumulated value crosses a configurable
  snapshot_threshold. Key behaviors:

  - HazardThreshold and MemoryPressure bypass threshold accumulation
    and trigger immediate captures for safety-critical scenarios.
  - Accumulated value resets to zero after each successful capture.
  - A periodic fallback timer (configurable in minutes) ensures
    liveness even when no triggers arrive.
  - Channel-based trigger delivery via emit_trigger() with bounded
    mpsc (capacity 64), returning false on backpressure.

Refactors capture logic into capture_from_provider() helper to
eliminate duplication between periodic and intelligent code paths.

Includes 11 new tests covering: trigger value mapping, immediate
trigger classification, channel delivery, threshold accumulation
(below/at/above), value reset after capture, shutdown behavior,
zero-threshold mode, periodic-mode trigger isolation, and channel
backpressure when full.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"ft-oegrb.3","title":"Storage track: append-only writer path + backend abstraction + FrankenSQLite integration","description":"## Track objective
Build the append-only persistence path for flight-recorder events, including backend abstraction, high-throughput writer path, and FrankenSQLite integration path with safe fallback.

## Why this track exists
Logging quality is bounded by write-path reliability and throughput. This track ensures durability under load while preserving rollout safety.

## Deliverables
- Storage abstraction for recorder log backends
- Baseline backend (for immediate bring-up)
- FrankenSQLite backend plan/integration path
- Crash recovery/replay and corruption handling strategy

## Acceptance criteria
- Sustained append throughput targets are met under concurrent writer load.
- Failure modes are explicit and exercised in automated tests.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:02:58.285668Z","created_by":"WildSpring","updated_at":"2026-02-12T06:09:16.717242Z","labels":["append-only","flight-recorder","frankensqlite","storage"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-oegrb.3","depends_on_id":"ft-oegrb","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:02:58.285668Z","created_by":"WildSpring","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"ft-oegrb.3","depends_on_id":"ft-oegrb.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:09:16.678028Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"ft-oegrb.3","depends_on_id":"ft-oegrb.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:09:16.717172Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"}]}
-{"id":"ft-oegrb.3.1","title":"Define recorder storage abstraction and backend-gated configuration","description":"## Context
The recorder must support multiple storage backends during rollout, including an immediate low-risk path and an advanced FrankenSQLite-backed path. We need a stable contract before implementation to avoid coupling capture, storage internals, and query/index pipelines.

## Scope
Define a versioned `RecorderStorage` abstraction (append, flush, checkpoint/read cursor, health, lag metrics) plus config/feature-gate surfaces selecting backend at runtime.

## Deliverables
- Trait/interface boundary and data contracts (event envelope + batch semantics)
- Backend selection config model with explicit default/fallback behavior
- Failure taxonomy and retry contract (retryable vs terminal)
- ADR notes on why this interface is shaped for high-throughput append-only ingestion

## Why this matters
A narrow, explicit storage contract prevents premature lock-in and lets us ship incrementally without rewriting capture or query layers.

## Acceptance
- Interface is sufficient for both local append-log backend and FrankenSQLite backend
- Error and durability semantics are explicit and testable
- Feature flags/config behavior are documented and deterministic","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:05:49.965804Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-12T06:05:50.659049Z","labels":["architecture","epic","flight-recorder","track-storage"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-oegrb.3.1","depends_on_id":"ft-oegrb.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:05:49.965804Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"ft-oegrb.3.1","depends_on_id":"ft-oegrb.1.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:05:50.618035Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"ft-oegrb.3.1","depends_on_id":"ft-oegrb.2.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:05:50.658992Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"}]}

 {"id":"ft-oegrb.3.2","title":"Implement ultra-fast append-only writer hot path for recorder events","description":"## Context
Flight recorder ingestion must remain ultra-low-overhead under swarm-scale burst traffic. The initial backend should be a minimal, append-only write path with predictable latency and minimal allocations.

## Scope
Implement the hot-path append writer (batched writes, bounded queues, durable flush policy) for canonical recorder events.

## Deliverables
- Append-only log writer implementation with batching and backpressure signals
- Tunable flush cadence/size thresholds with sane defaults
- Monotonic offset assignment and persisted write checkpoints
- Unit/integration tests for ordering and durability behavior

## Why this matters
This is the performance-critical spine of the recorder. If this path regresses runtime responsiveness, the feature fails regardless of search quality.

## Acceptance
- Sustains target ingest throughput without destabilizing watcher loop
- Maintains append ordering guarantees and crash-safe durability semantics
- Produces stable offsets consumable by indexers/replay tools","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:05:50.012648Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-12T06:05:50.754402Z","labels":["epic","flight-recorder","implementation","performance","track-storage"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-oegrb.3.2","depends_on_id":"ft-oegrb.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:05:50.012648Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"ft-oegrb.3.2","depends_on_id":"ft-oegrb.3.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:05:50.299584Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"ft-oegrb.3.2","depends_on_id":"ft-oegrb.2.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:05:50.69883Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"ft-oegrb.3.2","depends_on_id":"ft-oegrb.2.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:05:50.754321Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"}]}
 {"id":"ft-oegrb.3.3","title":"Integrate FrankenSQLite backend adapter for concurrent durable recorder writes","description":"## Context
User intent explicitly calls out evaluating/adapting `/dp/frankensqlite` (multi-writer WAL + self-healing ideas) for durable high-concurrency logging.

## Scope
Design and implement a FrankenSQLite-backed recorder adapter behind `RecorderStorage`, including concurrency, WAL settings, and corruption/recovery policy boundaries.

## Deliverables
- Adapter implementation mapping recorder event batches to FrankenSQLite schema
- Concurrency model (writer sharding, lock strategy, or queueing) documented and benchmarked
- WAL tuning + self-healing strategy notes (what is adopted vs deferred)
- Validation tests for correctness under concurrent writer stress

## Why this matters
This is the highest-upside durability/performance path and the main mechanism for learning from frankensqlite in production-adjacent conditions.

## Acceptance
- Adapter passes contract tests from storage abstraction
- Concurrency behavior is reproducible and does not violate ordering invariants
- Recovery behavior is documented with explicit operator expectations","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:05:50.068191Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-12T06:05:50.801332Z","labels":["epic","flight-recorder","frankensqlite","track-storage"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-oegrb.3.3","depends_on_id":"ft-oegrb.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:05:50.068191Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"ft-oegrb.3.3","depends_on_id":"ft-oegrb.3.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:05:50.34223Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"ft-oegrb.3.3","depends_on_id":"ft-oegrb.1.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:05:50.801271Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"}]}
 {"id":"ft-oegrb.3.4","title":"Implement crash-safe checkpointing and deterministic replay cursor semantics","description":"## Context
Indexer and replay systems require reliable continuation after process restarts/crashes. We need explicit checkpoints and idempotent resume semantics from storage.

## Scope
Implement write/checkpoint metadata and restart replay protocol so downstream consumers can resume from last committed offset without duplication or gaps.

## Deliverables
- Durable checkpoint format and atomic update protocol
- Resume/read APIs for indexers and replayers
- Duplicate suppression strategy where at-least-once replay is unavoidable
- Crash/restart integration tests

## Why this matters
Without deterministic replay/resume semantics, indexing quality and forensic trustworthiness degrade quickly.

## Acceptance
- Restart from crash resumes at deterministic point
- Replay APIs provide no silent gap behavior
- Duplicate handling strategy is explicit and tested","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:05:50.115436Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-12T06:05:50.435506Z","labels":["epic","flight-recorder","reliability","track-storage"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-oegrb.3.4","depends_on_id":"ft-oegrb.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:05:50.115436Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"ft-oegrb.3.4","depends_on_id":"ft-oegrb.3.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:05:50.393544Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"ft-oegrb.3.4","depends_on_id":"ft-oegrb.3.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:05:50.435384Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"}]}
 {"id":"ft-oegrb.3.5","title":"Define retention, partitioning, and archival lifecycle for append-only recorder data","description":"## Context
Full-fidelity recorder data can grow rapidly. Retention and partitioning choices must be first-class so we do not accumulate unbounded costs or operational risk.

## Scope
Define and implement retention windows, partition strategy (time/size), and archival/export hooks for append log segments.

## Deliverables
- Retention policy model (default + override scopes)
- Segment lifecycle logic (active, sealed, archived, purged)
- Tooling hooks for safe archival/export
- Tests proving retention operations preserve invariants and auditability

## Why this matters
Cost and operability determine whether this feature can remain always-on in real swarm usage.

## Acceptance
- Retention behavior is deterministic and observable
- Purge/archive operations do not corrupt offset continuity semantics
- Operators can reason about storage growth via documented policy","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:05:50.162884Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-12T06:09:16.606628Z","labels":["epic","flight-recorder","operations","track-storage"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-oegrb.3.5","depends_on_id":"ft-oegrb.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:05:50.162884Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"ft-oegrb.3.5","depends_on_id":"ft-oegrb.3.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:05:50.479191Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"ft-oegrb.3.5","depends_on_id":"ft-oegrb.8.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:09:16.60657Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"}]}
 {"id":"ft-oegrb.3.6","title":"Add deep storage-pipeline telemetry, diagnostics, and SLO-oriented metrics","description":"## Context
The writer path is mission-critical; without deep telemetry we cannot tune or safely roll out. We need first-class metrics and tracing for throughput, lag, failures, and durability state.

## Scope
Instrument storage pipeline with metrics/events/logging suitable for SLOs and incident triage.

## Deliverables
- Metrics for append rate, batch size, queue depth, flush latency, error classes, replay lag
- Structured diagnostics for backend health and checkpoint progression
- Alert recommendations for critical degradation patterns
- Documentation mapping metrics to likely remediation actions

## Why this matters
Observability is necessary both for performance tuning and for proving recorder trustworthiness in incidents.

## Acceptance
- Critical failure/lag states are externally visible
- Metrics are stable enough for automated thresholds
- Documentation allows operators to diagnose common failure modes","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:05:50.226861Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-12T06:05:50.570008Z","labels":["epic","flight-recorder","observability","track-storage"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-oegrb.3.6","depends_on_id":"ft-oegrb.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:05:50.226861Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"ft-oegrb.3.6","depends_on_id":"ft-oegrb.3.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:05:50.523344Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"ft-oegrb.3.6","depends_on_id":"ft-oegrb.3.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:05:50.569934Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"}]}
 {"id":"ft-oegrb.4","title":"Indexing track: Tantivy lexical indexing, incremental ingestion, rebuild tooling","description":"## Track objective
Design and implement lexical indexing over recorder logs using Tantivy with near-real-time incremental ingestion and robust rebuild/repair workflows.

## Why this track exists
The recorder only creates value if data is rapidly discoverable. Lexical search is the baseline reliability layer and must remain available regardless of semantic subsystem state.

## Deliverables
- Tantivy schema and analyzer strategy
- Incremental indexer pipeline from append-only log
- Query semantics for phrase, boolean, field filters, time windows
- Rebuild/checkpoint/repair tools

## Acceptance criteria
- Lexical search latency and correctness targets are documented and test-enforced.
- Index can be rebuilt from append-only logs without data ambiguity.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:02:58.381913Z","created_by":"WildSpring","updated_at":"2026-02-12T06:09:16.755958Z","labels":["indexing","lexical","search","tantivy"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-oegrb.4","depends_on_id":"ft-oegrb","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:02:58.381913Z","created_by":"WildSpring","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"ft-oegrb.4","depends_on_id":"ft-oegrb.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:09:16.755747Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"}]}
-{"id":"ft-oegrb.4.1","title":"Design versioned Tantivy schema for recorder text + metadata filters","description":"## Context
Lexical retrieval quality and index stability depend on getting schema decisions right early (field structure, analyzers, stored vs indexed fields, offset references, metadata filters).

## Scope
Design the canonical Tantivy schema for recorder events, including text fields, normalized metadata, and time/agent/pane filters.

## Deliverables
- Versioned Tantivy schema definition with migration notes
- Field-by-field rationale (why indexed/stored/tokenized)
- Analyzer/tokenizer strategy tuned for terminal text (commands, stack traces, mixed symbols)
- Golden tests for schema compatibility and queryability

## Why this matters
Schema drift or poor tokenization destroys search quality and makes reindexing expensive.

## Acceptance

 {"id":"wa-9l5","title":"Scheduled backup system: cron-style automatic backups with retention","description":"# Task: Scheduled Backup System

## Goal
Provide peace of mind through automatic, hands-off backups.

## Configuration
```toml
[backup.scheduled]
enabled = true

# Schedule (cron-like or simple keywords)
schedule = "daily"  # or "0 3 * * *" for 3 AM daily

# Retention policy
retention_days = 30
max_backups = 10  # Keep at most 10, even if newer than retention

# Destination
destination = "~/.local/share/wa/backups/"

# Options
compress = true
metadata_only = false  # Set true for quick backups

# Notifications
notify_on_failure = true
notify_on_success = false
```

## Behavior
- Backup runs as part of `wa watch` (if enabled)
- Uses same export logic as `wa backup export`
- Rotation: delete oldest when max_backups exceeded
- Retry: attempt 3 times with exponential backoff on failure
- Status visible in `wa status`:
  ```
  Scheduled backup: enabled (daily)
    Last backup: 2026-01-18T03:00:15 (45 MB)
    Next backup: 2026-01-19T03:00:00
    Backups kept: 8/10
  ```

## Implementation Notes
- Use tokio cron scheduler or simple timer
- Lock coordination: brief pause of writes during export
- Fail gracefully: never crash watcher if backup fails

## Testing
- Unit tests: schedule parsing, rotation logic
- Integration: trigger scheduled backup, verify rotation
- E2E: run watcher with backup enabled, verify files created

## Acceptance Criteria
- Backup runs on configured schedule
- Rotation respects both retention_days and max_backups
- Status shows last/next backup times
- Failures logged and optionally notified

 {"id":"wa-9lh","title":"E2E script: quick-fix suggestions (events + errors → actionable commands)","description":"# E2E script: quick-fix suggestions (events + errors → actionable commands)

## Goal
Prove end-to-end that wa always answers the user’s “now what?” question:
- **Unhandled events** include actionable suggestions (quick fix + preview + manual).
- **Errors** include actionable remediation (with context-aware hints).
- Suggestions are **safe, copy-pasteable**, and **machine-usable** (when requested via JSON).

This is the integration validation for the quick-fix system (`wa-bnm`) under the standard E2E harness.

## Why this matters
Quick-fix suggestions are a UX multiplier:
- They reduce cognitive load (“what command handles this?”).
- They teach the system (users learn correct workflows).
- They enable agent automation (robots can pick the recommended remediation).

## Key constraints (robustness)
- **Deterministic**: no fixed `sleep N`. Synchronize via wait-for/quiescence with explicit timeouts.
- **No `eval` / no shell interpolation** when validating executability.
  - If we need to *run* a suggestion, we must do so via a structured argv array (no shell).
- **No transcript leakage**: artifacts must not contain raw pane scrollback; suggestions must be redacted/safe.
- Use the standard harness contract (`wa-4vx.10.6`) and runner (`wa-4vx.10.11`).

## Test setup (E2E harness)
- Start a WezTerm mux server and spawn deterministic “dummy agent panes” (harness-owned).
- Run `wa watch` in an isolated E2E workspace with:
  - a known pattern pack enabled
  - a known set of panes that will emit markers to create *unhandled* events
  - stable output mode for machine parsing (`--format json` where applicable)

## Data contract (strongly recommended)
To make suggestions both copy-friendly and robot-executable, JSON outputs that include suggestions should carry both:
- `display`: human copy/paste string (best effort)
- `argv`: machine-executable argv array (preferred; avoids shells)

Example shape (illustrative):
```json
{
  "suggestions": {
    "quick_fix": {"display": "wa workflow run handle_compaction --pane 3", "argv": ["wa","workflow","run","handle_compaction","--pane","3"]},
    "preview":   {"display": "wa workflow run handle_compaction --pane 3 --dry-run", "argv": ["wa","workflow","run","handle_compaction","--pane","3","--dry-run"]},
    "manual":    {"display": "Ask the agent to re-read AGENTS.md and key files"}
  }
}
```

If `argv` is not available in v1, then E2E should still validate that `display` is strictly in a safe subset (no pipes/redirects/command substitutions), and **must not attempt execution**.

## Scenarios

### 1) Event suggestions appear and are complete
- Trigger a known unhandled event deterministically (via dummy pane output + pattern match).
- Run: `wa events --unhandled --format json`.
- Assert for each returned event:
  - `quick_fix` exists and is relevant to the event (e.g., suggests the correct workflow)
  - `preview` exists (`--dry-run` variant)
  - `manual` exists (human explanation)

### 2) Error suggestions for invalid inputs
- Run an operation that must fail deterministically (no WezTerm state needed), e.g.:
  - `wa send --pane 999 "hello" --format json` (or equivalent command expected to error)
- Assert error output includes:
  - a clear primary error message
  - “available panes” (context)
  - “did you mean …” when there’s a near match
  - suggested next steps (e.g., `wa status`)

### 3) Policy denial suggestions (context-aware)
- Create a deterministic unsafe pane state (fixture / dummy alt-screen mode).
- Attempt a send (`wa send …`) and assert the denial output includes:
  - the *reason* (e.g., AltScreen)
  - the *quick fix* (exit AltScreen)
  - the “why” trace command (e.g., `wa why denied --pane <id>`)

### 4) Fuzzy matching and typo recovery
- Trigger “pane not found” with a close pane id and assert “did you mean” suggestion.
- Trigger “unknown workflow” with a close name and assert fuzzy match suggestion.

### 5) Suggestion executability (no shell)
Pick one event suggestion that is expected to be a pure `wa …` remediation:
- Extract `suggestions.quick_fix.argv` (preferred).
- Execute it directly **without a shell** (argv exec).
- Assert the expected effect (e.g., a workflow execution is created OR a dry-run preview succeeds).

## Registry
- Register this case in the E2E case registry (`wa-4vx.10.20`) with prerequisites and default inclusion status.

## Artifacts
- `suggestions_output.json` (redacted event/error JSON)
- `suggestion_validation.json` (what we validated + pass/fail per rule)
- `copy_paste_execution.log` (argv executed + exit codes)
- `wa_watch.log`
- `events.jsonl`

## Logging contract
All steps log:
- case name + scenario name
- workspace + mux identifiers
- timeouts used
- extracted suggestion IDs (not full secrets)

Example:
```
[QUICKFIX_E2E] scenario=event_suggestions query=wa events --unhandled --format json
[QUICKFIX_E2E] event=session.compaction pane=3 quick_fix="wa workflow run handle_compaction --pane 3"
[QUICKFIX_E2E] scenario=exec argv=[wa workflow run handle_compaction --pane 3] rc=0
```

## Testing
- Determinism:
  - no fixed sleeps
  - every wait has a timeout and an error message pointing to artifacts
- Safety:
  - never execute suggestion strings via `eval`/`sh -c`
  - ensure no suggestion `display` contains shell metacharacters (`;`, `|`, `&&`, backticks, `$(`) unless explicitly allowed by a strict policy
- Privacy:
  - run the standard redaction scan (same logic as `wa-4vx.10.18`) over artifacts
  - assert suggestions do not contain secrets (device codes, tokens)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Unhandled events include quick_fix + preview + manual suggestions.
- [ ] Common errors include actionable fixes with context (available panes/workflows).
- [ ] Policy denials include remediation + why-trace hints.
- [ ] Executability is validated without shells (argv exec), or safely skipped if `argv` isn’t available yet.
- [ ] Artifacts/logs are sufficient to debug failures.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-18T19:13:11.641286474Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-30T05:49:21.228349238Z","closed_at":"2026-01-30T05:49:21.228259141Z","close_reason":"Added quickfix_suggestions E2E scenario, registered in e2e_test registry, updated checklist; validated registry alignment.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-9lh","depends_on_id":"wa-2vr1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:31Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-9lh","depends_on_id":"wa-mxzp","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:31Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-9lh","depends_on_id":"wa-4vx.10","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:31Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-9lh","depends_on_id":"wa-4vx.10.11","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:32Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-9lh","depends_on_id":"wa-4vx.10.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:32Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-9lh","depends_on_id":"wa-4vx.6.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:32Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-9lh","depends_on_id":"wa-bnm","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:32Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"wa-a0c","title":"[EPIC] Simulation Mode: mock WezTerm for testing, demos, and development","description":"# [EPIC] Simulation Mode

## Mission
Provide a complete mock WezTerm environment for testing, demos, and development without requiring a real WezTerm instance.

## Why This Matters
Current limitations:
- Testing requires WezTerm running
- CI cannot test WezTerm interactions
- Demos require live setup
- Development feedback loop is slow

Simulation mode enables:
- **CI Testing**: Full integration tests without WezTerm
- **Demos**: Self-contained demonstrations
- **Development**: Fast iteration without real terminals
- **Tutorial**: Safe sandbox for learning (wa-ogc.8 builds on this\!)

## Core Insight: Deterministic Testing
Real WezTerm interactions are inherently non-deterministic. Simulation provides:
- Reproducible test scenarios
- Controlled timing
- Predictable outputs
- Edge case injection

## Downstream Consumers
This simulation infrastructure is used by:
1. **CI/CD Tests** - Integration tests that run without WezTerm
2. **Demo Mode** - Self-contained presentations
3. **Tutorial Sandbox (wa-ogc.8)** - Safe learning environment for wa learn

## Scope

### Mock WezTerm CLI
Replace `wezterm` CLI calls with mock implementation:
```rust
pub trait WeztermInterface {
    fn list_panes(&self) -> Result<Vec<PaneInfo>>;
    fn get_text(&self, pane_id: u64, options: &GetTextOptions) -> Result<String>;
    fn send_text(&self, pane_id: u64, text: &str) -> Result<()>;
    fn spawn(&self, options: &SpawnOptions) -> Result<PaneInfo>;
}

// Real implementation
pub struct WeztermCli { ... }

// Mock implementation
pub struct MockWezterm {
    panes: HashMap<u64, MockPane>,
    scenarios: Vec<Scenario>,
}
```

### Scenario System
Define test scenarios declaratively:
```yaml
# scenarios/compaction_event.yaml
name: compaction_event
panes:
  - id: 0
    title: "Codex Agent"
    agent_type: codex
    initial_content: |
      codex> Working on task...
    events:
      - at: 1s
        append: |
          [Compaction Notice]
          Context window approaching limit.
          To continue, provide a summary.
```

### Event Injection
Inject events at controlled times:
```rust
impl MockWezterm {
    pub fn inject_output(&mut self, pane_id: u64, text: &str) {
        self.panes.get_mut(&pane_id)
            .unwrap()
            .append_output(text);
    }
    
    pub fn trigger_event(&mut self, event: &str) {
        // Append text that will trigger pattern match
    }
}
```

### CLI Integration
```bash
# Start wa in simulation mode
wa watch --simulate

# With specific scenario
wa watch --simulate --scenario compaction_event

# For demos
wa demo --scenario usage_limit_workflow
```

## Success Criteria
- CI can run full integration tests
- Demos run without WezTerm
- Development cycle faster
- Tutorial uses simulation for safe learning

## Testing Requirements
- Unit tests for mock implementation
- Integration tests comparing mock vs real behavior
- Scenario validation tests
- E2E tests for demo mode

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] MockWezterm implements full WeztermInterface
- [ ] Scenario system parses YAML definitions
- [ ] Event injection works with timing
- [ ] `wa watch --simulate` functional
- [ ] Demo mode shows scenarios
- [ ] CI uses simulation for tests

 {"id":"wa-oegrb.3","title":"Storage track: append-only writer path + backend abstraction + FrankenSQLite integration","description":"## Track objective
Build the append-only persistence path for flight-recorder events, including backend abstraction, high-throughput writer path, and FrankenSQLite integration path with safe fallback.

## Why this track exists
Logging quality is bounded by write-path reliability and throughput. This track ensures durability under load while preserving rollout safety.

## Deliverables
- Storage abstraction for recorder log backends
- Baseline backend (for immediate bring-up)
- FrankenSQLite backend plan/integration path
- Crash recovery/replay and corruption handling strategy

## Acceptance criteria
- Sustained append throughput targets are met under concurrent writer load.
- Failure modes are explicit and exercised in automated tests.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:02:58.285668Z","created_by":"WildSpring","updated_at":"2026-02-12T06:09:16.717242Z","labels":["append-only","flight-recorder","frankensqlite","storage"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.3","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:02:58.285668Z","created_by":"WildSpring","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.3","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:09:16.678028Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.3","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:09:16.717172Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"}]}
-{"id":"wa-oegrb.3.1","title":"Define recorder storage abstraction and backend-gated configuration","description":"## Context

Define a versioned `RecorderStorage` abstraction (append, flush, checkpoint/read cursor, health, lag metrics) plus config/feature-gate surfaces selecting backend at runtime.

 {"id":"wa-oegrb.3.3","title":"Integrate FrankenSQLite backend adapter for concurrent durable recorder writes","description":"## Context
User intent explicitly calls out evaluating/adapting `/dp/frankensqlite` (multi-writer WAL + self-healing ideas) for durable high-concurrency logging.

## Scope
Design and implement a FrankenSQLite-backed recorder adapter behind `RecorderStorage`, including concurrency, WAL settings, and corruption/recovery policy boundaries.

## Deliverables
- Adapter implementation mapping recorder event batches to FrankenSQLite schema
- Concurrency model (writer sharding, lock strategy, or queueing) documented and benchmarked
- WAL tuning + self-healing strategy notes (what is adopted vs deferred)
- Validation tests for correctness under concurrent writer stress

## Why this matters
This is the highest-upside durability/performance path and the main mechanism for learning from frankensqlite in production-adjacent conditions.

## Acceptance
- Adapter passes contract tests from storage abstraction
- Concurrency behavior is reproducible and does not violate ordering invariants
- Recovery behavior is documented with explicit operator expectations","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:05:50.068191Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-12T06:05:50.801332Z","labels":["epic","flight-recorder","frankensqlite","track-storage"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.3.3","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:05:50.068191Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.3.3","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.3.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:05:50.34223Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.3.3","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.1.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:05:50.801271Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"}]}
 {"id":"wa-oegrb.3.4","title":"Implement crash-safe checkpointing and deterministic replay cursor semantics","description":"## Context
Indexer and replay systems require reliable continuation after process restarts/crashes. We need explicit checkpoints and idempotent resume semantics from storage.

## Scope
Implement write/checkpoint metadata and restart replay protocol so downstream consumers can resume from last committed offset without duplication or gaps.

## Deliverables
- Durable checkpoint format and atomic update protocol
- Resume/read APIs for indexers and replayers
- Duplicate suppression strategy where at-least-once replay is unavoidable
- Crash/restart integration tests

## Why this matters
Without deterministic replay/resume semantics, indexing quality and forensic trustworthiness degrade quickly.

## Acceptance
- Restart from crash resumes at deterministic point
- Replay APIs provide no silent gap behavior
- Duplicate handling strategy is explicit and tested","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:05:50.115436Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-12T06:05:50.435506Z","labels":["epic","flight-recorder","reliability","track-storage"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.3.4","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:05:50.115436Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.3.4","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.3.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:05:50.393544Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.3.4","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.3.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:05:50.435384Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"}]}
 {"id":"wa-oegrb.3.5","title":"Define retention, partitioning, and archival lifecycle for append-only recorder data","description":"## Context
Full-fidelity recorder data can grow rapidly. Retention and partitioning choices must be first-class so we do not accumulate unbounded costs or operational risk.

## Scope
Define and implement retention windows, partition strategy (time/size), and archival/export hooks for append log segments.

## Deliverables
- Retention policy model (default + override scopes)
- Segment lifecycle logic (active, sealed, archived, purged)
- Tooling hooks for safe archival/export
- Tests proving retention operations preserve invariants and auditability

## Why this matters
Cost and operability determine whether this feature can remain always-on in real swarm usage.

## Acceptance
- Retention behavior is deterministic and observable
- Purge/archive operations do not corrupt offset continuity semantics
- Operators can reason about storage growth via documented policy","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:05:50.162884Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-12T06:09:16.606628Z","labels":["epic","flight-recorder","operations","track-storage"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.3.5","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:05:50.162884Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.3.5","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.3.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:05:50.479191Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.3.5","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.8.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:09:16.60657Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"}]}
